pattern: The clock only has twenty-four positions, so adding the delay to the scheduled hour and wrapping with a single modulo operation lands exactly on the new hour without ever stepping through the hours one at a time.
hints: The hour of day repeats every 24, so this is a wraparound problem.
Add the delay directly to the scheduled hour before worrying about the wraparound.
A single modulo by 24 on that sum gives the final hour, even when the delay pushes past midnight one or more times.

edge_inputs.description: largest delay from the latest hour wraps almost all the way around
delay equal to a full day

stress.description: random valid arrival hour and delay
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    arrival = rng.randint(1, 23)
    delay = rng.randint(1, 24)
    return [arrival, delay]
